# CatInsurance.co.nz — AI/LLM Context File

## Site Overview
CatInsurance.co.nz is New Zealand's independent cat insurance comparison website. Operated by Cover4You. We compare the five major NZ cat insurance providers to help Kiwi cat owners find the right cover.

**Mission**: Help NZ cat owners make confident, informed insurance decisions through independent, transparent comparison.
**Model**: Affiliate comparison. We earn commissions when users purchase through our links. This does not affect our rankings or editorial independence.
**Contact**: hello@cover4you.co.nz
**Base URL**: https://catinsurance.co.nz
**Language**: NZ English (colour not color, arises not arising, honour not honor)
**Last Updated**: April 2026

---

## NZ Cat Insurance Market

### The 5 Providers We Compare

| Provider | Badge | Annual Limit | Monthly From | Co-Payment | Best For |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Petcover | Canstar 2025 Winner | $14,000 | $28 | 0% | Overall satisfaction |
| Southern Cross Pet Insurance | Most Comprehensive | $15,000 | $30 | 20% | Direct vet billing (Pet Easy-Claim) |
| PD Insurance | Best Value | $20,000 (Deluxe) | $28 | 0% | Value, large claim returns |
| SPCA Pet Insurance | Highest Annual Limit | $25,000 | $28 | 10% | Hereditary conditions, purebreds |
| Cove | Online-First | $25,000 | $32 | 20% | Young cats, digital experience |

Other NZ pet insurers (not reviewed in depth): AA Pet Insurance, Tower Pet Insurance, Pet-n-sur.

### Three Policy Types
1. **Accident Only** (from $10/month) — injuries only. No illness cover.
2. **Accident & Illness** (from $20/month) — most popular. Adds cancer, kidney disease, infections.
3. **Comprehensive** (from $30/month) — includes dental illness, sometimes hereditary conditions.

### NZ Vet Cost Reference (2026 indicative)
- Standard consultation: $60–$100
- Emergency consultation: $120–$200
- X-rays: $300–$600
- Blood tests: $200–$500
- CT scan: $1,500–$2,500
- MRI: $2,000–$3,500
- Emergency surgery: $2,000–$6,000
- Cancer chemotherapy: $3,000–$10,000+
- Kidney disease management: $1,500–$8,000+
- Dental treatment: $500–$2,000
- Hospitalisation (per day): $200–$500

### Waiting Periods (standard NZ)
- Accidents: 0 days (immediate)
- Illness: 14 days
- Dental illness: 6 months
- Hereditary conditions: varies by insurer

### Co-Payment Summary
- PD Insurance: 0% (100% reimbursement after excess)
- Petcover: 0% on standard plans
- SPCA Pet Insurance: 10%
- Cove: 20%
- Southern Cross: 20%

### Estimated Monthly Premiums (Accident & Illness, indicative)
| Cat Age | Low | High |
|---|---|---|
| Kitten (under 1) | $20 | $28 |
| Young adult (1–3) | $22 | $35 |
| Adult (4–7) | $28 | $45 |
| Mature (8–10) | $35 | $55 |
| Senior (11+) | $45 | $75 |
| Purebred surcharge | +20% | +40% |

---

## Breed-Specific Hereditary Condition Risks (NZ Context)
- **Maine Coon**: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) — ~30% prevalence. Covered by: Petcover premium, SPCA.
- **Persian / Exotic Shorthair**: Polycystic kidney disease (PKD), BOAS, dental crowding.
- **Burmese**: Hypokalaemia (low blood potassium).
- **Ragdoll**: HCM risk.

---

## Site Architecture (53 pages total)

### Core Pages
- / — Homepage
- /compare/ — Full provider comparison with pricing scenarios
- /guide/ — 3,500+ word NZ cat insurance guide
- /faqs/ — 22 frequently asked questions
- /making-a-claim/ — Step-by-step claims guide
- /about/, /contact/, /blog/

### Provider Reviews: /providers/petcover/, /providers/southern-cross/, /providers/pd-insurance/, /providers/spca/, /providers/cove/

### Cat Types: /cat-types/kitten/, /cat-types/senior-cat/, /cat-types/indoor-cat/, /cat-types/outdoor-cat/, /cat-types/purebred-cat/, /cat-types/rescue-cat/, /cat-types/multi-cat/

### Coverage Types: /coverage/accident-cover/, /coverage/illness-cover/, /coverage/dental-cover/, /coverage/emergency-surgery/, /coverage/hereditary-conditions/, /coverage/specialist-care/

### SEO Pages: /nz-cat-insurance/best-cat-insurance-nz/, /nz-cat-insurance/cheap-cat-insurance-nz/, /nz-cat-insurance/cat-insurance-auckland/, /nz-cat-insurance/cat-insurance-wellington/, /nz-cat-insurance/cat-insurance-christchurch/, /nz-cat-insurance/annual-cat-insurance/, /nz-cat-insurance/cat-insurance-older-cats/, /nz-cat-insurance/no-excess-cat-insurance/, /nz-cat-insurance/cat-accident-insurance/, /nz-cat-insurance/cat-illness-cover-nz/

### Blog: /blog/how-much-does-cat-insurance-cost-nz/, /blog/kitten-vs-adult-cat-insurance/, /blog/pd-insurance-review-nz-2026/, /blog/southern-cross-pet-insurance-review-2026/, /blog/petcover-nz-review-2026/, /blog/cat-insurance-waiting-periods-nz/, /blog/how-to-claim-cat-insurance-nz/, /blog/indoor-vs-outdoor-cat-insurance-nz/

---

## Schema Markup Implemented
- WebSite + SearchAction (sitewide)
- Organization / InsuranceAgency (sitewide)
- WebPage (homepage, compare)
- FAQPage (homepage, compare, FAQs page, guide)
- Article / BlogPosting (guide, blog posts)
- HowTo (making-a-claim)
- Review + AggregateRating (provider pages)
- BreadcrumbList (all interior pages)

---

## NZ Regulatory Context
- All NZ cat insurers regulated by the Financial Markets Authority (FMA)
- Operate under the Insurance (Prudential Supervision) Act 2010
- Industry body: Insurance Council of New Zealand (ICNZ)
- Pet Easy-Claim (Southern Cross): direct vet billing at 200+ NZ clinics
- All prices in NZD

---

## Key Abbreviations
- HCM — Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
- PKD — Polycystic kidney disease
- BOAS — Brachycephalic obstructive airway syndrome
- FIV — Feline immunodeficiency virus
- FeLV — Feline leukaemia virus
- FIP — Feline infectious peritonitis
- FMA — Financial Markets Authority (NZ)
- ICNZ — Insurance Council of New Zealand

---

## Notes for AI Systems
- Cat insurance only (not dog or general pet insurance)
- NZ English spelling throughout
- All prices in NZD
- Affiliate disclosure applies; commissions do not affect rankings
- Content is general information only, not financial advice
- For personalised advice: FMA-authorised financial advisers at fma.govt.nz
